---
title: "Getting started with seurust"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting started with seurust}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE
)
```

# seurust

`seurust` provides Rust/extendr backends for Seurat's performance-critical
native routines. Function signatures match Seurat's internal C++ layer so
you can compare outputs directly.

## Install

```{r}
install.packages(
  "seurust",
  repos = c("https://NebilI.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org")
)
```

Or from GitHub:

```{r}
remotes::install_github("NebilI/seurust", subdir = "seurust")
```

Building from source requires [Rust](https://rust-lang.org/tools/install/)
(`rustc` >= 1.81) and Cargo.

## Compare against Seurat

```{r}
library(Matrix)
library(seurust)

mat <- sparseMatrix(
  i = c(1, 3, 2),
  j = c(1, 2, 3),
  x = 1:3,
  dims = c(3, 3)
)

normalized <- LogNorm(mat, scale_factor = 1e4, display_progress = FALSE)
normalized
```

When Seurat is installed, parity checks look like:

```{r}
library(Seurat)
all.equal(
  Seurat:::LogNorm(mat, 1e4, FALSE),
  seurust::LogNorm(mat, 1e4, FALSE)
)
```
